phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q only — last-hit with autos and save the stun strictly for his step-ups. Your egg is NOT a license to int level 1.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q-E online — a landed stun now means a real chunk. Trade only when his engage tool is down; your mana hates wasted rotations.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full basic kit: W cuts the lane in half. Wall his path to the wave when your jungler shadows — free stun setups.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Hit Lost Chapter timing and the mana war ends. Chip Anivia with max-range Q threats and bank Manaflow stacks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Glacial Storm changes everything — your wave-clear is instant and the Q-R-E execute is real. Anivia must respect every stun line now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Seraph's/RoA spike: your storm stays on longer than his patience. Zone him off the wave and call jungle dives onto the chill slow.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: You are now a teamfight wall: storm the choke, wall the flank, and double-damage E anything that touches the slow zone.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your wall-frost poke control beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your wall-frost poke control out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — wall-frost poke control edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your wall-frost poke control beat Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Glacial Storm changes everything — your wave-clear is instant and the Q-R-E execute is real. Aurelion Sol must respect every stun line now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control edge out your wall-frost poke control on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.why: Respect window: through levels 4-5 the wave-dash blade stacks beat your wall-frost poke control cleanly. Trade only off Irelia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Glacial Storm changes everything — your wave-clear is instant and the Q-R-E execute is real. Irelia must respect every stun line now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Seraph's/RoA spike: your storm stays on longer than his patience. Zone him off the wave and call jungle dives onto the chill slow.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: You are now a teamfight wall: storm the choke, wall the flank, and double-damage E anything that touches the slow zone.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q only — last-hit with autos and save the stun strictly for his step-ups. Your egg is NOT a license to int level 1.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q-E online — a landed stun now means a real chunk. Trade only when his engage tool is down; your mana hates wasted rotations.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full basic kit: W cuts the lane in half. Wall his path to the wave when your jungler shadows — free stun setups.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Hit Lost Chapter timing and the mana war ends. Chip Pantheon with max-range Q threats and bank Manaflow stacks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Glacial Storm changes everything — your wave-clear is instant and the Q-R-E execute is real. Pantheon must respect every stun line now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Seraph's/RoA spike: your storm stays on longer than his patience. Zone him off the wave and call jungle dives onto the chill slow.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items your wall-frost poke control beat the point-click spear bullying in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Pantheon answer you, not the other way around.
